task_yaml.task:
Someone pasted this chart into a slide with the title and the data-source
line cropped off, and I don't want to cite it without knowing where it really
comes from. The image is here:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/crop.jpg

Please figure out what the chart shows and trace it back to its authoritative
source. Specifically I need:
- what the chart is measuring (and the units),
- the canonical chart page it comes from (the publisher's own interactive
  chart, not a news article that reused it),
- the UNDERLYING data source/provider that the chart itself is built on
  (this is the part the cropped-off footer hid),
- a link to download the underlying data.

Save your conclusion and the evidence chain (how you identified the chart and
traced both the chart page and the underlying data provider) to
`/tmp_workspace/results/chart_provenance.json`, plus notes/screenshots under
`/tmp_workspace/results/`.
